subsystems.purpose: A deterministic, fail-closed security kernel that sits between the agent (planner/executor) and the host OS. It classifies every action payload into one of three zones (GREEN auto-run / YELLOW human-approval / RED blocked), refuses anything not provably safe, prevents directory-escape and secret leakage, and records every security-relevant decision in a tamper-evident hash-chained ledger. It is explicitly built as a kernel the LLM "cannot reason its way past": classification is pure pattern matching with no model judgement in the control path.
subsystems.architecture: Layered, single-responsibility modules with a strict, ordered pipeline. gateway.classify() runs checks in danger-descending order: regex injection -> optional vector injection shield -> secret scan -> destructive -> network egress -> env/secret mutation -> shell/interpreter escape -> shell composition metachars -> scope check -> YELLOW caution allowlist -> GREEN safe allowlist -> else RED. "Most dangerous match wins" and the terminal else is RED (allowlist model, not blocklist). validate_command() maps zones to actionable GatewayDecision (ALLOW/REQUIRE_HUMAN/BLOCK) and applies the per-session YELLOW rate limit. scope_lock canonicalises every path-like shell word via pathlib.resolve() (symlink-following) and checks containment against declared SCOPE_ROOTS (default training_ground). secret_scanner does a dual named-regex + Shannon-entropy scan with non-reversible redaction. audit_logger appends SHA-256 hash-chained entries to an isolated SQLite DB, redacting secrets before hashing. injection_shield is an opt-in embedding-similarity second layer (cosine against curated injection strings). The Executor (aios/core/executor.py) is the consumer: it calls validate_command, audits every outcome, then runs GREEN/approved commands with shell=False structured argv, a sanitised env (secret-name vars + HOME/USERPROFILE/Bedrock token stripped), scope-locked cwd, bounded output, and a hard timeout. The API (aios/api/main.py) exposes /security/classify, /audit/verify, /execute, /approval/req and installs the vector shield at startup when configured.
capabilities: Three-zone deterministic classification (Zone enum GREEN/YELLOW/RED) with fixed, repeatable verdicts — no LLM in the path (gateway.classify, gateway.py:283)

Fail-closed everywhere: empty/non-str input, unknown commands, and ANY internal exception resolve to RED via a top-level try/except returning RED (gateway.py:296-297, 348-350); proven by test_fail_closed_on_internal_exception
Allowlist execution model: only echo/pwd (GREEN) and an explicit caution set (YELLOW) survive; everything else is RED — 'Unknown command is not on the auto-execute allowlist' (gateway.py:348)

Path scope locking via real-path resolution: relative traversal (../../etc), absolute/drive paths (C:\Windows), '~' home refs, and symlink escapes all blocked (scope_lock.is_path_in_scope / command_stays_in_scope)
Shell-operator-aware tokenisation: splits on ;|&<>` BEFORE shlex so a path glued by a redirect/semicolon (echo x>/home/..., cat foo;/etc/passwd) is isolated and scope-checked (scope_lock.py:29,149)

Dual secret scan: named credential regexes (OpenAI sk-, AWS AKIA-family, GitHub ghp_, Bearer, assigned password/secret/api_key) PLUS Shannon-entropy >= 4.0 over >=20-char tokens, with non-reversible <REDACTED:NAME:8hexhash> replacement (secret_scanner.scan_and_redact)

SHA-256 hash-chained audit ledger: current_hash = SHA256(prev||ts||actor||payload||zone), genesis = 64 zeros; O(n) verify_chain detects both payload tampering (hash mismatch) and linkage breaks (previous_hash mismatch) and reports broken_at (audit_logger.py:115-270)

No-secret-persistence in the audit log: payload is redacted BEFORE it is hashed and stored, so the chain stays internally consistent and credentials never enter the ledger (audit_logger.log_action:158-160; test_secret_is_redacted_before_persistence)

Per-session sensitive-action rate limit with optional cross-process SQLite backing (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), session ids stored only as SHA-256 (RateLimiter); after MAX_RED_ACTIONS_PER_SESSION YELLOWs, fresh human re-auth required

Opt-in vector injection shield: embeds input and compares cosine vs 10 curated injection paraphrases (>= threshold => RED), fail-SAFE (embedder error => False) so a model failure never weakens the regex layer or blocks everything (injection_shield.VectorInjectionShield)
RED can never be granted by approval: execute_approved re-classifies and refuses RED even after human one-click approval (executor.py:435-443) — stricter than a typed-token override

Executor process hardening: shell=False structured argv, secret-name env stripping, scope-locked cwd, bounded output draining, hard timeout, optional Docker backend (--network none, --read-only, --cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, non-root uid)
sophistication: The shell-operator-aware scope tokeniser (scope_lock.command_stays_in_scope) is genuinely subtle: it splits on shell metacharacters first so a glued absolute path becomes its own word, then shlex-tokenises each segment with posix=False to keep Windows backslashes literal, AND deliberately checks a relative tool path like .venv\Scripts\python.exe intact to avoid a documented false-positive where a mid-word separator was mis-read as the rooted \Scripts\python.exe. This is a real bug-fix-shaped design, not naive substring matching, and is regression-tested both ways.

Defence-in-depth with correct fail-direction asymmetry: the gateway is fail-CLOSED (errors => RED) while the injection shield is fail-SAFE (errors => False). This is the right call — a flaky optional ML layer must never block every command, and it can only ADD blocks on top of the deterministic regex layer, never subtract. The contract is enforced and tested (test_vector_shield_is_fail_safe_on_embedder_error).

The audit chain is a real Merkle-style append-only ledger with cross-process integrity: a process-level threading.Lock plus SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE extends the head-read+append critical section across worker processes, so concurrent appenders cannot fork the chain. test_concurrent_appends_keep_one_valid_chain drives 4 threads x 20 appends and verifies a single valid 80-entry chain.

verify_chain correctly distinguishes full vs partial verification: a full walk (from_id<=1) anchors on the genesis hash so even tampering with entry 1 is caught, while a partial range anchors on the first row's stored previous_hash. It returns a precise broken_at id and a reason string distinguishing linkage break from payload tamper.

Redact-before-hash is the non-obvious correctness insight that makes no-secret-persistence and chain-validity coexist: hashing the redacted text keeps the chain self-consistent while guaranteeing the raw secret never touches disk. Many naive implementations would hash the raw payload and break this.

Entropy + regex dual secret scan is the right belt-and-suspenders: named regexes catch known formats with good labels; the Shannon-entropy pass (threshold 4.0, calibrated to separate random base64/hex from natural language) catches novel high-randomness tokens the regexes miss, with plain English provably not flagged.

The classifier ordering is a deliberate security lattice ('most dangerous category that matches wins'), and the GREEN set is an explicit 2-pattern allowlist (echo/pwd) rather than a blocklist — the safe-by-default posture that makes 'unknown => RED' the dominant outcome.

Shell-escape patterns specifically target interpreter -c/-e/-Command/encodedcommand hatches (python -c, node -e, powershell -Command, cmd /c, bash -c) that would otherwise smuggle arbitrary writes/network behind an innocent-looking executable, aligned with the executor's shell=False argv boundary.
subsystems.completeness: This is solid, production-shaped engineering, not scaffolding. All five modules are fully implemented, internally consistent, and wired end-to-end into the Executor and the FastAPI layer. 53 security/audit tests pass (1 skipped: Windows symlink creation needs privilege) and they cover the load-bearing claims directly: fail-closed-on-exception, empty=>RED, injection (direct + vector), scope escape (absolute/relative/symlink/home/glued-redirect/glued-semicolon/bare-parent), secret detect+redact, entropy, rate-limit re-auth, hash-chain genesis linkage, tamper detection with broken_at, redact-before-persist, invalid-zone fail-closed, and concurrent-append chain integrity. The fail-closed contract is real and verified, not aspirational. The executor honestly self-documents its own boundary: host mode is NOT OS/container isolation and approved code runs as the backend OS user — and it provides a genuinely hardened Docker backend (network none, read-only, cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, non-root) as the stronger boundary. The vector injection shield is correctly scoped as opt-in/lazy so the default gateway stays pure-regex and torch-free.
gaps: The regex injection and destructive pattern sets are necessarily finite blocklists for the RED-via-pattern cases. This is mitigated, not eliminated, by the allowlist model (unknown => RED) and the optional vector shield, so a missed injection phrasing does not silently auto-execute — it just may not be labelled 'injection'. Still, the curated vector set is only 10 strings and is off by default.

command_stays_in_scope only scope-checks tokens that 'look like paths' (contain a separator, start with .., or have a drive prefix); a bare program name is intentionally skipped. This is deliberate and the dangerous-execution cases are caught by the shell-escape/composition/destructive layers instead, but scope enforcement itself relies on those other layers for non-path-shaped commands.

Host execution mode (the local-first default, APPROVED_EXECUTION_BACKEND=host) explicitly cannot guarantee process-tree containment or OS isolation — approved arbitrary code runs as the backend user. This is clearly documented as a known limitation with the container backend offered as the remedy; it is a stated trade-off, not a hidden hole.

The entropy threshold (4.0) and vector cosine threshold (0.6 default) are tuned heuristics; like all such thresholds they carry inherent false-negative/false-positive trade-offs at the margins, though the chosen values are well-justified in comments and tested against representative cases.

audit_logger has no built-in external anchoring (e.g. periodic notarisation of the head hash off-box), so an attacker with full write access to the SQLite file who recomputes the ENTIRE chain forward from a tampered entry could produce a self-consistent forged ledger. Detecting that requires comparing the head hash against an out-of-band record. This is the standard limit of a local hash chain without an external root of trust, and is inherent to the threat model (local-first, single laptop) rather than an implementation defect.
subsystems.testCoverage: Strong and behaviour-focused. tests/test_security.py (parametrised zone mapping, determinism, empty=>RED, direct + vector injection incl. fail-safe-on-embedder-error and a real semantically-novel paraphrase test, full scope-escape matrix, secret/entropy detection, fail-closed-on-monkeypatched-exception, interpreter/nested-shell escapes, shell-composition, rate-limit re-auth, durable cross-worker limiter + legacy-session-id migration) and tests/test_audit.py (genesis linkage, tamper => broken_at, deterministic 64-hex hashing, redact-before-persist with chain still valid, invalid-zone AuditError, empty-chain validity, 4-thread x 20 concurrent-append single-valid-chain). Verified run: 53 passed, 1 skipped (Windows symlink privilege), in ~38s. Tests assert the security CONTRACTS (fail-closed direction, no-secret-persistence, tamper detection precision, RED-not-grantable) rather than incidental implementation details.

keyFiles.role: The kernel: classify() runs the ordered danger-descending pipeline and is fail-closed (try/except => RED, gateway.py:349-350); validate_command() maps zones to ALLOW/REQUIRE_HUMAN/BLOCK and enforces the per-session YELLOW rate limit; RateLimiter offers optional cross-process SQLite backing with SHA-256 session keys; GREEN is a 2-pattern allowlist and the terminal default is RED (gateway.py:348).

Directory-escape defence: is_path_in_scope resolves real paths (symlink-following) and checks containment; command_stays_in_scope splits on shell operators then shlex-tokenises (posix=False) and scope-checks each path-like word intact, blocking absolute/traversal/~/glued-path escapes (scope_lock.py:125-177).
Dual named-regex + Shannon-entropy (threshold 4.0, >=20 chars) secret detection with non-reversible <REDACTED:NAME:8hex> replacement (scan_and_redact, shannon_entropy).

SHA-256 hash-chained tamper-evident ledger in an isolated SQLite DB: log_action redacts-before-hash and appends under threading.Lock + BEGIN IMMEDIATE (cross-process chain lock); verify_chain does O(n) genesis-anchored verification reporting broken_at and distinguishing linkage break vs payload tamper.

Opt-in embedding-similarity second injection layer: VectorInjectionShield.is_injection embeds input and takes max cosine vs 10 curated injection strings against a threshold; fail-SAFE (any error => False) so it only adds RED verdicts, never weakens the regex layer.

The primary consumer/enforcement point: validate_command-gates and audits every command, runs GREEN/approved code with shell=False structured argv, sanitised env, scope-locked cwd, bounded output and hard timeout; execute_approved re-classifies and refuses RED even post-approval; provides the hardened DockerRunner isolation backend.

Live wiring: /api/v1/security/classify, /audit/verify, /execute (issues approval token on YELLOW), /approval/req (approve => execute_approved, RED still refused; reject => audited); installs the VectorInjectionShield at startup via set_injection_shield when configured.

Single source of truth for security tunables: SCOPE_ROOTS (default training_ground), MAX_RED_ACTIONS_PER_SESSION, AUDIT_GENESIS_HASH (64 zeros), INJECTION_VECTOR_* thresholds, APPROVED_EXECUTION_BACKEND + container caps, command/output size bounds.

subsystems.subsystem: Cognition core — the think→plan→act→verify loop (aios/core/* + agentic composition in aios/agents/tool_agent.py, wired into aios/api/main.py)
subsystems.purpose: Run a supervised, local-first cognitive loop where a weak local LLM (Ollama) can actually act on a workspace — plan, read, edit/create files, run commands, verify, and self-correct — while a deterministic security kernel and a confidence gate keep a human in control. The design's load-bearing thesis is "Trust Evidence, Not the Model": no LLM claim (of safety, of confidence, of success, of approval) is ever taken at face value; deterministic code re-derives or re-proves it.
subsystems.architecture: Clean separation of mechanism (deterministic, tested) from cognition (LLM, untrusted). Components depend on a minimal LLMClient/ChatClient Protocol (aios/core/llm.py), so the entire loop is driven in tests by injected fakes with no Ollama and no model — that is why the loop is testable at all. Composition: AlignmentInterpreter (alignment.py) builds an advisory UnderstandingFrame from dialogue; model_selector.py routes the turn to an installed model by inferred task; ToolAgent (tool_agent.py) runs a bounded reason→act→observe loop calling tools that all funnel through Executor (executor.py) → gateway.classify (security/gateway.py) → audit_logger (hash-chained ledger); Verifier (verifier.py) judges outcomes by exit code; failures feed a reflection hook into MistakeMemory; Planner (planner.py) decomposes goals and runs every step through the independent confidence_filter gate; SelfApplyEngine (self_apply.py) is the guarded path for the OS to patch its own source. main.py confirms this is wired end-to-end in production (lines 775, 960-994, 1269-1470), not just in tests.
capabilities: Bounded agentic loop (ToolAgent.run, tool_agent.py:627) over Ollama function-calling with a step cap (DEFAULT_MAX_ITERS=5), yielding transport-agnostic event dicts the API forwards as SSE

Robust tool-call recovery (_extract_text_tool_calls, tool_agent.py:409) handling FOUR observed local-model prose shapes: native tool_calls, fenced JSON, bare back-to-back JSON objects, and ReAct 'Action:' narration — with an allowlist so prose can never invent a dispatcher route

Deterministic 3-zone security gateway (gateway.classify, gateway.py:283) with strict precedence injection→secret→destructive→network→env→shell-escape→shell-composition→scope→caution→safe; fail-closed (empty/unknown/exception → RED); an explicit GREEN allowlist (unknown commands are refused, not shell-defaulted)

Resumable human-in-the-loop approval: a YELLOW tool pauses the turn (human_required event); the frontend re-calls with the exact command/edit/creation whitelisted; approved writes are pre-applied deterministically before the model speaks (_pre_apply_grants, tool_agent.py:790) — fixing the 'dropped grant' replay bug

Server-issued, expiring, single-use, session-bound approval capabilities (ApprovalStore, approvals.py) — SQLite-durable, multi-process safe via BEGIN IMMEDIATE, persisting only SHA-256 digests of token+session and refusing to persist credential-like payloads

Scope-locked, env-sanitized, structured-argv (shell=False) command execution with hard timeout and bounded streamed output (executor.py _bounded_run/_default_runner); optional locked-down ephemeral Docker backend (DockerRunner: --network none, --read-only, --cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, non-root, pids/mem/cpu caps, tmpfs noexec)

Evidence-based verification (Verifier.verify, verifier.py): exit code is authoritative pass/fail, parsed counts only enrich; fail-closed (BLOCKED/TIMEOUT/ERROR = FAIL); a genuine failure fires the reflection hook closing execute→verify→reflect

Force-verify-after-write (_auto_verify, tool_agent.py:881): after an approved edit/create lands, the loop AUTONOMOUSLY runs the file's sibling pytest so an authoritative PASS/FAIL — not the model's narration — anchors the next turn; Python-write-with-no-sibling-test is explicitly reported UNVERIFIED
Confidence-gated planning (Planner.plan + confidence_filter.filter_steps): steps below 0.72 escalate to human review independent of security zone (orthogonal gates)

Evidence-based confidence calibration (Planner._calibrate, planner.py:147): self-reported confidence is adjusted only by VERIFIED external memory — verified mistake lessons (negative, bounded -0.4), historical success rate (±0.15), and verified skill trails (positive, capped) — so repetition alone can't manufacture confidence

Deterministic task-aware model routing (model_selector.select_model) with require_tools to keep the agentic loop off reasoning-only/base/embedding/vision models even when the request reads like reasoning

Advisory alignment framing (AlignmentInterpreter.understand): a validated, redacted, explicitly NON-authoritative UnderstandingFrame, plus a deterministic communication policy that narrowly decides proceed/state_assumptions/ask, and user-authored corrections that override interpretation for communication ONLY

Guarded self-application (SelfApplyEngine.apply, self_apply.py): snapshot → git apply --check → audit-before-write → single-file-confined git apply → independent two-snapshot integrity check → gated verify → auto-rollback; RED frozen core refused; no-self-approval enforced both structurally (no agent apply tool) and defensively (approver≠proposer)
Tamper-evident SHA-256 hash-chained audit ledger (audit_logger.py) with O(n) verify_chain, secret redaction before hashing, cross-process BEGIN IMMEDIATE append lock, fail-closed AuditError
sophistication: The orthogonal-gates design is genuinely principled: security zone (deterministic, gateway) and confidence (planner/filter) are independent axes — a GREEN-zone step still escalates if the planner is unsure, and human approval can NEVER authorize a RED action (executor.execute_approved re-classifies and refuses RED even when approved). The gateway is explicitly a kernel the model 'cannot reason its way past.'

Defense-in-depth against the weak-local-model failure mode is the real intellectual core. The recurring danger — a small model narrating success it didn't achieve, or getting the last word — is countered structurally: force-verify-after-write, exit-code-authoritative verdicts, fail-closed everywhere, and feeding the authoritative verdict back into the conversation so the model's NEXT turn is anchored to PASS/FAIL not its own prose.

SelfApplyEngine is unusually careful for a self-modifying system. The independent _expected_after re-applies the approved diff to an isolated temp copy and byte-compares against the real write — catching ANY unintended extra change. The no-self-approval guard is correctly identified as STRUCTURAL (the agent has only a 'propose' tool; 'apply' is reachable only from a human HTTP endpoint), with approver≠proposer as a labeled defensive second layer. Audit-before-write is fail-closed: no ledger entry → no write.

The confidence calibration is honest by construction: only VERIFIED evidence moves the number (relevant_verified queries WHERE verification_status='verified'; SkillMemory counts only verification-backed successes), bonuses are bounded so a memory trail 'can never single-handedly clear the human-review gate,' and the lesson-confirmation path promotes a pending lesson only when its EXACT failed command later succeeds in the same live run — not on unrelated success.

_extract_text_tool_calls is battle-hardened against real local-model behavior (four distinct prose shapes, llama3.1 'parameters' vs 'arguments' keying, ast.literal_eval for single-quoted Python-dict fences that never executes code) — clearly written from live observation, not speculation, yet still funneled through the same allowlist + dispatcher so robustness never widens the attack surface.

The approval capability model is textbook: the browser may approve but cannot author a payload by posting it back — the server records the exact pending action and issues an opaque single-use token; redeem→grant builds a replay chain for resumable multi-step approval; everything is digest-only and durable across restart/workers.

The container runner (DockerRunner) and the host runner are honestly distinguished in the module docstring: host mode 'is not an OS/container isolation boundary' and 'cannot guarantee process-tree containment' — the system tells the truth about its own weaker mode rather than overclaiming.
subsystems.completeness: SOLID AND PROVEN, not scaffolded. This is a real, coherent cognitive loop, end-to-end wired in production (aios/api/main.py composes AlignmentInterpreter→model selection→ToolAgent with planner_llm/self_analysis_llm/executor/approved_runner/reflection hooks) and backed by deep, behavior-level tests driven through injected fakes. Test depth on the cognition core specifically is substantial: test_tool_agent.py (63 tests), test_security.py (32), test_executor.py (20), test_self_apply.py (18), test_model_selector.py (15), test_alignment.py (13), test_approvals.py (8), test_planner.py (7), test_verifier.py (7), test_confidence.py (2) — and these inject fakes so they exercise the FULL gateway+audit+verify pipeline deterministically, which is the right way to test this. The deterministic mechanism layer (gateway, audit chain, approvals, scope-lock, executor, self-apply) is the genuinely solid foundation; the LLM-dependent layer (planner JSON parsing, alignment frame extraction, tool-call recovery) is hardened against malformed/adversarial model output with field-by-field fallback and fail-low defaults.
gaps: By design, not defect: GREEN auto-execute is a TINY allowlist (only echo and pwd in _SAFE_PATTERNS), and _default_runner even special-cases echo/pwd. So in HOST mode the 'autonomous execution' surface is essentially trivial commands; anything real (pytest, edits, installs, git) is YELLOW and requires human approval. The loop's autonomy is deliberately narrow — this is a supervised OS, not an autonomous one — but it does mean the unattended GREEN path does very little.

Strong OS-level execution isolation exists ONLY in container mode (DockerRunner). The default APPROVED_EXECUTION_BACKEND='host' runs approved arbitrary-code commands as the backend OS user inside the scope-locked cwd with a sanitized env — the docstring is candid that this 'is not an OS/container isolation boundary' and timeouts 'cannot guarantee process-tree containment.' The container backend requires Docker present (ensure_available), which on this Windows host is a real operational dependency, so the strongest boundary is opt-in, not the default.

Planner/alignment quality is bounded by the local model: planning is ADVISORY only (the plan tool never executes; main.py:775 builds a Planner but the loop still routes real actions through the gate), and self-reported step confidence is only meaningful after calibration against accumulated VERIFIED memory — on a cold DB (no verified lessons/skills/history) calibration is a no-op and the gate runs on raw model self-report. This is acknowledged in MEMORY (castes/stigmergy 'architecture proven / 7B-model-limited').

Verification pass/fail count parsing (_parse_counts) is pytest/jest-summary-shaped regex; for runners with different output the exit code still governs correctness (good, fail-closed), but the enriched counts/delta degrade to 0/-0.1. Not a correctness gap, a richness gap.

_auto_verify only verifies Python writes that have a sibling test_<name>.py (or are themselves test_*.py); other languages and untested Python are correctly reported UNVERIFIED rather than verified — honest, but means cross-language writes get no automated evidence.
subsystems.testCoverage: Deep and behavior-focused, not smoke tests. The cognition-core test files alone contain ~218 test functions; the suite leans on dependency injection (fake LLMClient/ChatClient, injected runners, injected audit sinks, temp SQLite DBs) so the entire gateway→executor→audit→verify→reflect pipeline and the approval replay chain run deterministically without Ollama, a shell, or the real ledger. There are golden-fixture tests (tests/golden/) for the self-analysis path and a test_brain_growth.py exercising the memory-calibration loop. The testing strategy is itself evidence of the architecture's quality: the loop is testable precisely because cognition is isolated behind Protocols and every deterministic guard is a separately exercisable unit. (Counts were read from the test files directly; I did not execute the suite in this session, so I report structure/coverage breadth rather than a fresh green run — the repo MEMORY notes 89 tests passing historically and this subset is far larger.)

keyFiles.role: The actual agentic loop. ToolAgent.run (line 627) is the reason→act→observe driver; _extract_text_tool_calls (409) recovers prose tool calls; _auto_verify (881) forces evidence after writes; _pre_apply_grants (790) makes approvals replay-safe; _dispatch (964) is the single tool router that also enforces caste tool subsets.

Scope-locked, env-sanitized, structured-argv command execution with timeout + bounded output (_bounded_run, _default_runner); execute()/execute_approved() enforce that human approval cannot authorize RED; DockerRunner is the hardened isolated backend.

The deterministic, fail-closed 3-zone security kernel. classify() (283) is pure pattern matching with strict precedence and a GREEN allowlist; validate_command() (353) adds per-session YELLOW rate limiting. This is the gate the model cannot reason past.
Evidence-over-model verification: exit-code-authoritative pass/fail, fail-closed on BLOCKED/TIMEOUT/ERROR, bounded negative confidence delta, fires the reflection hook on genuine failure.
Goal→confidence-scored step tree via LLM; _calibrate (147) adjusts self-reported confidence using ONLY verified mistake/skill/history evidence; partitions via the independent confidence gate.
The second, orthogonal human-in-the-loop gate: any step below CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD (0.72) escalates regardless of security zone.

Server-issued, expiring, single-use, session-bound approval capabilities; SQLite-durable + multi-process safe; digest-only storage; refuses to persist credential-like payloads; redeem/grants build the resumable replay chain.

SelfApplyEngine — the guarded path for the OS to patch its own aios/ source: snapshot→check→audit-before-write→confined apply→independent two-snapshot integrity→gated verify→auto-rollback, with structural + defensive no-self-approval and RED frozen-core refusal.
Pure, deterministic task-aware model routing; require_tools keeps the agentic loop off non-tool-calling (reasoning/base/embedding/vision) models; infer_task classifies the request.

AlignmentInterpreter + UnderstandingFrame: validated, redacted, explicitly non-authoritative interpretation with a deterministic communication policy and user-authored corrections that affect communication only, never authority.
The minimal LLMClient Protocol + OllamaClient (urllib-only, no deps) providing complete/chat/stream_complete/list_models — the seam that makes the whole loop injectable and testable without a model.

SHA-256 hash-chained tamper-evident ledger; secret redaction before hashing; O(n) verify_chain; cross-process append lock; fail-closed AuditError that every write path treats as 'unrecorded action = refused action.'

Proves production wiring: composes AlignmentInterpreter, select_model/infer_task routing, Planner(llm), and ToolAgent with planner_llm/self_analysis_llm/executor/approved_runner/reflection hooks (lines 775, 960-994, 1269-1470) — the loop is live, not test-only.

subsystems.subsystem: Agent layer (aios/agents/ + the core/security siblings it orchestrates)
subsystems.purpose: A supervised, security-gated, memory-driven agent layer for a local-first AI-OS. It lets a (typically weak, local Ollama) model actually ACT on a codebase — read, run commands, edit/create files, verify, plan, and audit its own code — but every consequential action is funneled through a deterministic three-zone gateway, human approval, snapshots, audit ledger, and evidence-based verification. The unifying thesis throughout is "trust the evidence, not the model": the model proposes, but authoritative machinery (gateway/verifier/git-integrity) decides.
subsystems.architecture: Five cooperating agents over shared core services. (1) ToolAgent (tool_agent.py) is a bounded reason->act->observe loop. It is transport-agnostic: ToolAgent.run() is a generator yielding plain event dicts (tool_call/tool_result/tool_blocked/human_required/text/code/done/error) that the API forwards as SSE and tests assert on directly — chat client and Executor are injected, so the whole loop is driven by a scripted fake with no Ollama and no shell. (2) ReflectionAgent converts a genuine failure into a structured JSON lesson persisted in the L4 MistakeMemory. (3) role_pass.run_role_pass is a conductor that runs planner->coder->reviewer castes SEQUENTIALLY, each a FRESH ToolAgent over the same executor with a caste prompt and a mechanically-enforced tool subset; the only handoff media are the shared message list and on-disk artifacts (deliberately no message bus, no concurrency, no new state). (4) RollbackEngine snapshots/restores an ISOLATED git work-tree in the sandbox. (5) SelfAnalysisAgent statically indexes+diagnoses the system's own aios/ package (T0/T1), proposes fix diffs (T2), with the separate SelfApplyEngine (core/self_apply.py) performing the guarded T3a apply. All writes/exec route through Executor -> gateway.classify (deterministic regex, fail-closed RED default) -> scope_lock -> audit ledger; verification routes through Verifier; planning through Planner with a confidence gate.
capabilities: Bounded tool loop (DEFAULT_MAX_ITERS=5) exposing 9 real tools: read_file, read_directory, execute_terminal, edit_file, create_file, verify, plan, self_analyze, propose_fixes — each dispatched in ToolAgent._dispatch with a (output,status,failed) contract

Genuine security gating: GREEN commands auto-run, YELLOW pauses the WHOLE turn with a human_required event and is RESUMABLE (frontend re-calls /api/generate with approved_commands/approved_edits/approved_creations), RED is always refused even when listed as approved (Executor.execute_approved re-classifies and denies RED)

Path-traversal defense on every read: _resolve_within canonicalizes via Path.resolve() and rejects ../, absolute, and symlink escapes; file content is secret-scrubbed (scan_and_redact) before the model ever sees it

Resumable approval that survives model drift: approved_edits/approved_creations are keyed by FILEPATH (not the full triple), so on the replayed turn the agent applies exactly what the human approved, not the model's regenerated args. _pre_apply_grants lands granted writes deterministically BEFORE the model speaks (the fix for the 'dropped-grant' bug)

Force-verify-after-write (_auto_verify): when an approved edit/create lands, the loop AUTONOMOUSLY runs the file's sibling pytest through the gated Verifier and feeds the authoritative PASS/FAIL back into the conversation so the weak model cannot confabulate success; a .py file with no sibling test is reported UNVERIFIED, non-Python writes are left untouched — all fail-closed

Reflection-on-failure: a genuine execute_terminal failure (non-zero/timeout/launch error, NOT a security block) fires on_failure -> ReflectionAgent.reflect, which asks the LLM for a structured lesson, validates it, and writes it to mistake_pool; recurrences increment a counter instead of duplicating (record_or_increment under BEGIN IMMEDIATE)

Evidence-gated lesson promotion: a lesson is only promoted pending->verified when the EXACT same failed command later succeeds in the same live run (_confirm matches on command equality); unrelated later success is never treated as proof

Role-pass castes with reviewer authority derived from VERIFIER EVIDENCE ONLY: run_role_pass computes review_failed() from per-target [VERIFY FAIL]/[VERIFY PASS] verdicts, never from model prose; a failing review buys exactly one coder retry + one final review; a coder leg that wrote nothing skips review

Caste tool restriction enforced mechanically, not by prompt: allowed_tools filters the advertised specs AND ToolAgent._dispatch denies any disallowed name first-line — which also catches prose-recovered calls

Self-Analysis T0/T1: deterministic static analysis (stdlib ast/tokenize) producing missing_test, smell, todo, complexity, uncovered findings as facts (no model); radon used when present else an AST branch-count proxy; coverage join reads an existing .coverage read-only

Self-Analysis T2: propose_open drafts unified-diff fixes via an injected COMPLETION llm, scrubs secrets, and flips findings open->proposed with proposed_zone/proposed_by — never edits source, never applies

Self-Analysis T3a apply (SelfApplyEngine.apply): the only path that writes aios/ itself — snapshot -> git apply --check -> audit-before-write -> single-file-confined git apply -> independent two-snapshot integrity check -> gated verify -> auto-rollback on any failure; RED frozen core (aios/security/*) refused; no-self-approval enforced both structurally (no agent apply tool) and defensively (approved_by != proposed_by)

Isolated git rollback (RollbackEngine): snapshots a dedicated work-tree at the sandbox scope root with the git DATABASE out-of-tree under ROLLBACK_DIR; hard-refuses to operate on the project root; FileLock-serialized; rollback = git reset --hard + clean -fd verified against the target sha

Robustness against weak local models: _extract_text_tool_calls recovers a SINGLE allowlisted tool call emitted as prose in four observed shapes (fenced JSON, bare leading {, 'parameters' vs 'arguments' keying, ReAct 'Action: name {...}'), tolerating Python-style mappings via ast.literal_eval — but can never invent a route outside TOOL_SPECS
sophistication: The resumability design is the standout: YELLOW approval pauses a streaming generator turn and resumes it by REPLAY (re-running the whole turn) rather than mid-stream continuation. Keying grants by filepath and pre-applying them before the model speaks (_pre_apply_grants) is a deliberate, non-obvious fix for the failure mode where the replayed model regenerates slightly different args and a granted write silently vanishes. The noop status on byte-identical re-creates / already-present edits makes replay idempotent.

Defense-in-depth that does NOT trust prompts: caste tool subsets and the read/write scope split are enforced at the execution dispatcher (_dispatch denies first-line; scope_lock.is_path_in_scope does pure containment after canonicalization), so a model — or a prose-recovered call — physically cannot exceed its caste even if the system prompt is ignored.

'Trust evidence, not the model' is implemented as actual control flow, not a slogan: reviewer verdicts and post-write verification are computed from exit codes + parsed counts via the gated Verifier; the Verifier trusts the runner's EXIT CODE as authoritative (so an incidental 'error' substring in passing output cannot flip a verdict) while parsed counts only enrich the delta.

Fail-closed is consistent and layered: gateway defaults unknown commands to RED (explicit auto-execute allowlist), scope_lock fails closed on any resolution error, _auto_verify treats a missing test as UNVERIFIED and a blocked/unrunnable verify as FAIL, edit/create abort if snapshot OR audit fails (no unprotected, no unlogged write), and audit-before-write ordering is mirrored in both edit_file/create_file and the T3 apply engine.

SelfApplyEngine's two-snapshot integrity check is genuinely careful: it independently re-applies the approved diff to a fresh copy of the original bytes in an isolated temp dir and requires the real on-disk result to byte-match — catching any unintended extra change before deciding to keep or roll back. Combined with single-file diff confinement (_diff_paths must equal exactly {target_path}) and re-derivation of the RED zone from target_path (never trusting the stored zone), this is real supply-chain-grade caution for self-modification.

The no-self-approval guarantee is structural first, check second: there is deliberately NO agent tool to apply (the agent only has propose_fixes), so applying is reachable ONLY via a human-invoked endpoint; the approved_by != proposed_by check is explicitly the defensive second layer. That is the correct security posture (don't rely on a comparison you can also remove a tool to enforce).

Stable finding identity across runs: finding_fingerprint = sha256(path|type|symbol) deliberately EXCLUDES volatile evidence (line numbers), and write_report reconciles by fingerprint within the analyzed scope — inserting new, updating evidence on existing-open, closing vanished-open, and never disturbing a decided (proposed/applied/...) row. This keeps T2 from re-proposing the same fix N times and preserves the decision/audit lineage. The radon and proxy paths share the same bare-name symbol so switching analyzers UPDATES rather than duplicates.

Confidence calibration in the Planner adjusts the model's self-reported confidence using ONLY external verified evidence (verified lessons' confidence_delta, historical success rate, verified skill strength) with bounded adjustments, and the skill bonus is capped so a trail can never single-handedly clear the human-review gate — confidence and security are kept orthogonal.

The atomic write helper (_atomic_write_text) uses fsync + os.replace for edits and os.link (atomic no-clobber) for creates, preserving create_file's no-overwrite refusal even under races — a level of durability care most agent loops skip entirely.
subsystems.completeness: Solidly built and proven end-to-end, not scaffolded. The mechanisms described in the docstrings are real and exercised by 148 passing tests across the eight agent-layer suites I ran (test_tool_agent alone has 63 tests; role_pass 7, reflection 9, rollback 9, self_analysis 31, self_apply 18, verifier 7, planner 7). I traced every claimed control path into real code: the gateway is genuinely deterministic regex with a RED default; RED-under-approval refusal is real in Executor.execute_approved; scope_lock canonicalization and the read/write scope split are real; _auto_verify, _pre_apply_grants, filepath-keyed grants, evidence-gated lesson promotion, the role-pass reviewer-from-verifier-evidence logic, RollbackEngine's project-root refusal + out-of-tree git db, SelfAnalysis T0/T1 determinism, T2 propose, and the T3a apply engine's snapshot->check->audit->apply->integrity->verify->rollback sequence are all present and tested. This is the work of someone who has hit the real failure modes (dropped grants, model prose tool-calls, weak-model confabulation, double-reflection) and engineered specific fixes. The dependency injection discipline (every external — chat, executor, planner llm, snapshot, audit — is injectable) is consistent and is what makes the suite trustworthy rather than mock-theater.
gaps: dead_code finding is explicitly deferred in SelfAnalysisAgent (would need the vulture dependency) — documented, not silently missing.

write_report's scope LIKE pattern is unescaped (noted in-code as a v1 caveat): a scope path containing SQL _ or % would be treated as wildcards. Real scopes ('aios', test 'pkg') contain neither, so it is currently safe but is a latent sharp edge.
Two identically-named functions in one module, or two identical TODO lines in one file, collapse to a single fingerprint (acknowledged v1 fingerprint limitation).

Host-mode execution is honestly NOT a container/OS isolation boundary — approved arbitrary-code commands run as the backend OS user. The Executor docstring says so plainly and a DockerRunner (network none, read-only rootfs, cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, non-root uid, pids/memory/cpu limits) exists as the stronger backend, selected via config; isolation strength is therefore a deployment choice, not a code gap.

The textual-tool-call fallback intentionally recovers only the FIRST tool call per message (one-call-at-a-time), so a local model that genuinely wants to batch calls in prose is serialized — a deliberate trade for protocol safety, not a bug.

Reflection/propose quality is bounded by the local model: the STRUCTURE (validation, clamping, persistence, lifecycle) is robust, but the usefulness of a given lesson or proposed diff depends on the 7B-class model generating it — the system is honest that LLM commentary is non-authoritative and gated.
subsystems.testCoverage: Strong and genuine. I ran the eight relevant suites under the project venv: 148 passed in ~10s. Tests use a scripted fake chat client and a fake process Runner but drive the REAL security gateway, REAL scope_lock, REAL Verifier and REAL DB reconciliation — so the blocking/approval/verification behavior under test is the production behavior, not mocked away (the test files state this philosophy explicitly and the code confirms it via constructor injection). Coverage spans the hard cases: RED-under-approval refusal, traversal/scope denial, dropped-grant pre-apply, prose tool-call recovery, force-verify-after-write, evidence-gated promotion, role-pass reviewer-from-evidence + single-retry, rollback project-root refusal, and the full self-apply refuse/rollback/apply matrix (18 tests).

keyFiles.role: The supervised reason->act->observe loop. ToolAgent.run (generator) + ._dispatch route 9 tools; _auto_verify forces evidence after writes; _pre_apply_grants + filepath-keyed approved_edits/creations make YELLOW approval resumable; _reflect/_confirm wire failure-learning; _extract_text_tool_calls hardens against weak-model prose.

Sequential planner/coder/reviewer caste conductor. run_role_pass enforces tool subsets mechanically, derives the reviewer verdict from verifier evidence only (review_failed), and grants exactly one coder retry; handoff is the shared message list + sandbox artifacts.

Failure -> structured lesson. ReflectionAgent.reflect validates/clamps LLM JSON and persists via MistakeMemory.record_or_increment; confirm_lesson promotes pending->verified; recall_relevant feeds lessons back into later turns.
Isolated git snapshot/restore over the sandbox scope root with an out-of-tree git db; hard-refuses the project root; FileLock-serialized create_snapshot/rollback/list_snapshots.

The marquee Self-Analysis module T0/T1/T2: deterministic ast/tokenize indexing+diagnosis (findings as facts), fingerprint-based reconcile (write_report), and LLM-drafted fix proposals (propose_open) that never touch source. classify_target is the single source of truth for the RED frozen-core zone shared with T3.

Self-Analysis T3a guarded apply — the only writer of aios/ source. SelfApplyEngine.apply: snapshot -> git apply --check -> audit-before-write -> single-file git apply -> independent two-snapshot integrity check -> gated verify -> auto-rollback; no-self-approval + RED-frozen-core refusal.

Blueprint stage-8 evidence layer the agents rely on. Verifier.verify runs through the gated Executor, trusts exit code as authoritative, fires the reflection hook on genuine failure, and fails closed on blocked/timeout/launch errors.

The deterministic, fail-closed three-zone kernel (classify/validate_command) the entire agent layer cannot reason past: injection->secret->destructive->network->env->shell-escape->scope->caution->safe, unknown=RED; RateLimiter caps YELLOW per session.

Scope-locked, env-sanitized, structured-argv (shell=False) executor with bounded output and a hard timeout. execute_approved re-classifies and refuses RED even after approval; optional DockerRunner is the stronger isolation backend.

subsystems.purpose: A multi-store, neuroscience-inspired "hippocampus" for a local-first supervised AI-OS. It separates volatile working state, a durable chronological episodic log, a vector-backed semantic knowledge store, an entity-relation fact store with contradiction detection, a structured mistake/lesson pool, and an evidence-gated consolidation path that promotes only verified or human-approved knowledge into trusted long-term memory. Retrieval is a hybrid BM25 + FAISS + temporal-decay ranker. The throughline design principle is "trust evidence, not the model": nothing becomes authoritative without verification or human approval, and recall is tiered as trusted vs. unverified at the prompt layer.
subsystems.architecture: Four-plus-layer store model, each a thin CRUD/lifecycle facade over SQLite (single file aios_memory.db) plus, for L3, a FAISS index. L1 Working memory (working.py / WorkingMemory) is RAM-only, never persisted, thread-safe (RLock) per-session KV + ordered history. L2 Episodic (episodic.py / EpisodicMemory) is the append-only chronological turn log; session ids stored as SHA-256 digests, content secret-scrubbed; recency + time-window queries over idx_episodic_session / idx_episodic_time. L3 Semantic (semantic.py / SemanticMemory) keeps durable knowledge chunks each bound 1:1 to a FAISS vector via IndexIDMap where semantic_memory.id IS the vector id (embeddings.py / VectorIndex, HNSW, inner-product over L2-normalised MiniLM vectors = cosine). L3b Facts (facts.py / SemanticFacts) stores (subject,predicate,object) triples with active/superseded lifecycle. L4 Mistake pool (mistake.py / MistakeMemory) stores structured post-mortems with pending/verified/superseded lifecycle. A separate durable conversation-alignment store (conversation.py / ConversationStateStore) persists advisory understanding frames and a versioned correction ledger. consolidation.py / MemoryConsolidator is the gate that promotes verified lessons + human-approved facts into trusted L3. retrieval.py / hybrid_search is the cross-store reader. db.py centralizes tuned connections (WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, FK on, Row factory) and an idempotent schema bootstrap + in-place migration engine. The whole thing is wired into the chat turn in aios/api/main.py: _recall_memory + _recall_lessons + _recall_skills assemble context before a turn, _record_episode + _index_turn persist after, and the turn's verified verdict drives consolidation.
capabilities: Working memory: thread-safe (RLock) session-scoped KV store + ordered conversation buffer, strict per-session isolation, RAM-only by design (WorkingMemory.set/get/append_message/history/clear/sessions)

Episodic memory: secret-scrubbed, SHA-256-session-keyed append-only turn log with chronological recent() (selects newest by id DESC then reverses) and since() time-window queries; legacy raw-session-id rows remain readable during migration

Semantic memory: write path embeds text and adds the vector under the SQLite row's own id, keeping relational + vector stores in lock-step; exact active duplicates are consolidated (occurrence_count++, last_seen_at bump) without adding a second vector; repetition never upgrades verification status

Crash-consistent semantic writes: if embedding/vector-add/persist fails AFTER the row is committed, the row is DELETEd to prevent DB/index drift; VectorIndex.persist writes a temp file then atomically replaces (tmp.replace)

Hybrid retrieval: real Okapi BM25 (rank-bm25) over a FAISS candidate pool + cosine similarity from FAISS + exponential temporal decay, combined as alpha*BM25 + beta*FAISS + gamma*exp(-lambda*dt) with weights from config (0.25/0.45/0.30, lambda=0.05/hr); returns per-component sub-scores for explainability (RetrievalResult)

Candidate-pool expansion: hybrid_search retrieves top_k*candidate_multiplier from FAISS, and if too few survive the active-rows DB filter it doubles the pool up to index.size to maintain recall past superseded/inactive vectors

Semantic facts: contradiction detection — before committing (subject,predicate,object), checks for an active fact with same subject+predicate but different object and refuses to silently commit, returning a structured contradiction result with conflict id/object for human/reflection routing; exact duplicates idempotent; reconcile() supersedes and replaces under human approval

Consolidation gate: MemoryConsolidator.run/consolidate_lesson/promote_fact/reconcile_fact promote ONLY verified mistake-pool lessons and human-approved facts into verified L3, and supersede the vector memories of superseded lessons/facts; promote_fact/reconcile_fact hard-require a non-empty approved_by

Conversation alignment: ConversationStateStore persists the latest advisory frame per session and a full correction ledger with optimistic-concurrency (expected_revision) supersede-and-insert semantics, clear-to-base restoration, and redacted newest-first history

Mistake pool learning: structured post-mortems (error_type/root_cause/fix_applied/lesson_text), confidence_delta clamped to [-1.0, 0.0] so an unverified lesson can only reduce planner confidence, deterministic lexical relevant_verified() recall, record_or_increment() recurrence collapsing

Secret hygiene everywhere on write: scan_and_redact applied in episodic, semantic, facts, mistakes, and conversation stores before persistence; session ids stored only as SHA-256 digests across episodic/conversation

Idempotent schema bootstrap + live migration engine (db.py init_memory_db/_migrate): ALTER-TABLE backfills, in-place rehashing of legacy raw session ids, dedup of exact-normalized semantic rows, partial unique indexes (one active row per content_hash, one open finding per fingerprint, one active correction per session)
sophistication: This is genuinely a multi-store cognitive model, not a log. The separation maps deliberately to a memory taxonomy (L1 working RAM / L2 episodic / L3 semantic / L3b facts / L4 mistakes) and each store has its own lifecycle state machine (unverified->verified->superseded for semantic; pending->verified->superseded for mistakes; active->superseded for facts; active->superseded->cleared for corrections).

The id-coupling fix is a real engineering insight: embeddings.py uses faiss.IndexIDMap so semantic_memory.id IS the vector id, explicitly removing the fragile positional coupling (FAISS ordinal == row id) the legacy code relied on. Inner-product over L2-normalised vectors makes search scores exact cosine similarities.

Crash-consistency reasoning is real and documented at the call site (semantic.py add): row committed first to mint a stable vector id, then on any post-commit failure the row is deleted; the comment correctly argues an in-process orphan vector is harmless because retrieval joins candidates back to existing active DB rows and filters it out. The compensation is paired with atomic temp-file-replace index persistence.

Concurrency is handled at multiple levels and actually tested: a process-local threading.Lock plus a cross-process filelock.FileLock around semantic writes (with index reload() under the lock to avoid lost updates), BEGIN IMMEDIATE transactions to serialize the contradiction-check-then-insert in facts (test_concurrent_fact_writers_cannot_commit_contradictions) and the recurrence-check-then-insert in mistakes, and VectorIndex mtime-based _refresh_if_changed so long-lived readers observe another process's persisted index.

The retrieval formula is the blueprint's R(q,m,t) implemented faithfully with REAL Okapi BM25 (rank-bm25) rather than naive term overlap, normalized to [0,1], cosine clamped to [0,1], and UTC-correct exponential recency decay (naive-UTC parsing to match SQLite CURRENT_TIMESTAMP). RetrievalResult exposes every sub-score, so ranking is explainable, not a black box.

Evidence-gated promotion is the conceptual core and it is enforced, not decorative: consolidation.py refuses to promote pending/superseded lessons and unapproved facts, and main._recall_memory tiers recall into 'VERIFIED TRUSTED MEMORY' vs 'UNVERIFIED PRIOR CHAT MEMORY (use only as a lead, never as evidence)'. Indexed chat turns are deliberately written as unverified and never auto-upgraded by repetition.

relevance.skill_signature_v2 is a thoughtful piece of design: arc-level identity = goal-token key + argument-stripped tool sequence, with a documented rationale (secret-redaction noise lives in argument tails so exact-step signatures fragment the same arc; arc length is verdict signal so collapsing different-length arcs would launder failures). The '||' separator is chosen so v2 keys can never collide with the legacy single-'|' space.

The migration engine in db.py is unusually mature for a 2-week build: it backfills content hashes, merges exact-normalized duplicate semantic rows (summing occurrence_count), rehashes legacy raw session ids in place, and consolidates fragmented procedural-skill trails by recomputing status from DIRECT counts only — deliberately DELETE-ing duplicate semantic rows but only SUPERSEDE-ing skill rows (with a lineage pointer) because trail rows are irreplaceable verifier evidence. That divergence is reasoned, not accidental.

Partial unique indexes encode invariants in the DB itself: one active semantic row per content_hash, one active correction per session, one open self-analysis finding per fingerprint, one active skill per signature_v2 — pushing correctness down to the storage engine instead of trusting application code.

Optimistic concurrency on conversation corrections (record_correction's expected_revision check raising 'conversation correction changed; retry') is a genuinely careful detail for a per-session advisory ledger, with secret-scrubbed JSON payloads validated to remain structured.
subsystems.completeness: SOLID and proven, not scaffolded. Every store in scope is fully implemented end-to-end (write path, read path, lifecycle transitions, secret/session hygiene) and wired into the live chat turn in aios/api/main.py (recall before, persist+consolidate after, verdict-driven outcome). The subtle correctness properties — crash-consistent semantic writes, concurrent contradiction prevention, decay timing, duplicate consolidation, candidate-pool expansion past inactive vectors, migration idempotence — are not just claimed in docstrings but covered by targeted tests (tests/test_memory.py 30 tests, tests/test_brain_growth.py 31 tests, plus tests/test_relevance.py and tests/test_data_isolation.py). The FAISS/embedding layer uses production-grade components (HNSW, IndexIDMap, sentence-transformers MiniLM, lazy singleton so import stays cheap). The error-handling discipline at the integration layer is correct: recall/index/episode are all best-effort and never fatal to the chat. This reads like deliberately engineered infrastructure with a coherent guiding philosophy, not an MVP stitched together.
gaps: No automatic consolidation scheduler: there is no 'sleep'/'dream'/nightly background job. Consolidation is triggered explicitly — per-turn for a just-verified lesson (main.py around line 1253) and on-demand via the POST /api/v1/memory/consolidate endpoint / MemoryConsolidator.run(). This is a deliberate supervised-design choice (promotion is evidence/approval gated), but the neuroscience 'sleep consolidation' metaphor is manual, not autonomous.

Working memory (L1) has no TTL/eviction despite the docstring referencing a 'Session TTL'. It is a plain in-process dict that grows until clear(session_id) is called or the process exits; there is no time-based expiry or size cap.

No forgetting/compaction of episodic or semantic stores: episodic is append-only with no pruning, and semantic rows are only ever superseded (soft-deleted) or merged on exact-hash duplicate, never aged out. Temporal decay affects ranking only, not retention. Long-running installs grow monotonically.

BM25 is computed over only the FAISS candidate pool, not the global corpus, so IDF statistics are local to the candidate set (a pragmatic latency/recall tradeoff, documented, but it means pure-lexical matches that FAISS never surfaces as candidates can be missed). The bm25 normalization also divides by the candidate-set max rather than a corpus-wide statistic.

Contradiction detection in facts.py is exact-string on (subject,predicate); it has no semantic/normalization layer, so 'User prefers dark mode' vs 'user likes dark theme' would not be detected as the same predicate or as a conflict. Detection is robust for normalized triples but naive for free-text phrasing.

_index_turn writes the raw Q->A turn into semantic memory as unverified 'chat' on every answered turn (gated only by config.INDEX_CHAT), so unverified chat can accumulate and dominate the candidate pool over time; the only counter-pressure is the verified/unverified tiering at recall, not any cap or decay on how much unverified chat is stored.
subsystems.testCoverage: Strong and behavior-focused. The in-scope modules are exercised by ~65+ targeted tests: tests/test_memory.py (30) covers working-memory isolation, episodic chronological ordering + secret redaction + session-id hashing + legacy migration, conversation state/correction revision/stale-revision rejection/redaction, semantic add + hybrid-search ranking + empty-index short-circuit + candidate expansion past inactive vectors + crash-compensation on embed/persist failure + write serialization + index reload + long-lived-reader refresh + recency decay, and the full facts contradiction/idempotence/concurrent-writers/reconcile suite. tests/test_brain_growth.py (31) covers consolidation gating (verified-only lessons, approved-only facts), duplicate consolidation without a new vector, empty-content refusal, and the skill_signature_v2 redaction-noise/arc-length/migration behaviors. tests/test_relevance.py and tests/test_data_isolation.py add lexical-scoring and path-isolation/empty-index-no-model-load coverage. The tests target exactly the non-obvious correctness properties (concurrency, atomicity, decay, dedup) rather than trivial getters, which is the right signal that the hard parts are actually proven.

keyFiles.role: L3 semantic store; SemanticMemory.add implements embed+index lock-step, exact-duplicate consolidation, and the row-delete crash-compensation; lifecycle ops promote/supersede/supersede_text
hybrid_search: BM25(rank-bm25)+FAISS-cosine+exp-decay ranker with candidate-pool expansion and per-component RetrievalResult sub-scores
EmbeddingModel (lazy MiniLM singleton, L2-normalised) + VectorIndex (HNSW IndexIDMap, atomic temp-replace persist, mtime reload) — the id-coupling fix lives here
MemoryConsolidator: the evidence/approval gate that promotes only verified lessons + human-approved facts into trusted L3 and supersedes vectors of retired knowledge
SemanticFacts: entity-relation triples with BEGIN-IMMEDIATE contradiction detection (find_conflict/add_fact) and human-approved reconcile()
ConversationStateStore: durable advisory frames + optimistic-concurrency correction ledger (record_correction/clear_correction/refresh_active_correction)
L4 MistakeMemory: structured post-mortems, confidence_delta clamped to [-1,0], deterministic relevant_verified recall, record_or_increment recurrence collapsing
L2 EpisodicMemory: secret-scrubbed, SHA-256-session-keyed append-only turn log with recent()/since() queries
L1 WorkingMemory: RLock-guarded RAM-only per-session KV + history (no persistence, no TTL/eviction)
Deterministic lexical scoring (relevance, content_hash) and the arc-level skill_signature_v2 (goal-tokens + argument-stripped tool sequence)
Tuned connection factory (WAL/NORMAL/FK/Row) + idempotent init_memory_db and the live _migrate engine (backfills, rehash, dedup, partial-unique invariants, trail consolidation)
Declarative DDL for all layers; CHECK constraints encode each store's lifecycle state machine; partial indexes encode active-uniqueness invariants
Integration: _recall_memory (tiered trusted/unverified), _record_episode, _index_turn, and verdict-driven consolidation wire the stores into the live chat turn
30 tests proving concurrency, atomicity, redaction, decay, dedup and candidate expansion for the in-scope stores
31 tests proving consolidation gating and skill-signature/migration semantics

subsystems.subsystem: Learning memory (procedural skills / curriculum / stigmergic development trails / mistake pool / embeddings / alignment evaluation)
subsystems.purpose: A verification-gated "brain growth" layer that lets a supervised local AI-OS genuinely learn from its own run history without ever letting unverified narration calibrate future behavior. It accumulates four kinds of durable, cross-session evidence — reusable verified workflows (procedural skills as stigmergic pheromone trails), a held-out-gated developmental curriculum, structured failure post-mortems (mistake pool), and developmental outcome statistics — plus a semantic embedding index and a diagnostic-only human-alignment evaluation store. The hard invariant across every module is: only authoritative verifier-backed outcomes ([VERIFY PASS]/[VERIFY FAIL]) may change what the brain trusts; everything else is advisory.
subsystems.architecture: SQLite-backed (WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, FK on, Row factory) declarative schema in aios/memory/schema.sql, bootstrapped idempotently by aios/memory/db.py:init_memory_db with in-place ALTER-TABLE migrations in db.py:_migrate. Each learning concern is a thin store class over one table: SkillMemory (procedural_skills), CurriculumManager (curriculum_tasks), DevelopmentTracker (development_events), MistakeMemory (mistake_pool), AlignmentEvaluationStore (alignment_observations). Cross-cutting helpers live in aios/memory/relevance.py (deterministic lexical scoring: tokens/relevance/signature/skill_signature_v2 — NO LLM judge) and aios/security/secret_scanner.scan_and_redact (every text field is scrubbed before persistence). The semantic layer (embeddings.py) is a separate lazy stack: EmbeddingModel (thread-safe singleton over sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2, L2-normalised) + VectorIndex (FAISS IndexIDMap over HNSW, inner-product == cosine, mtime-based cross-process reload, atomic temp-file persist). The loop is closed in aios/core/planner.py:Planner._calibrate (reads verified skills/lessons/dev-history to adjust step confidence BEFORE the human-review gate) and aios/api/main.py:record_outcome (writes development+skill+curriculum evidence AFTER the turn's verifier verdict; _recall_skills injects verified workflows into agent context, and record_reuse credits/stains the recalled trails). The writer is the verifier verdict, not the model.
capabilities: Procedural skill acquisition with promotion gate: SkillMemory.record_attempt (skills.py:67) promotes a workflow to 'verified' only at >=3 verified successes AND >=0.8 success rate; a later verified failure recomputes the rate and demotes it back to 'candidate' (proven by test_skill_memory_promotes_after_repeated_verified_success_and_regresses).

Arc-level skill identity / anti-fragmentation: skill_signature_v2 (relevance.py:52) keys a trail on goal-tokens + the argument-STRIPPED tool sequence, so the same workflow with redaction noise in a path argument reinforces ONE trail, while a 'flail' arc with extra repeated steps stays a DISTINCT failure record (test_redaction_noise_consolidates_to_one_signature and test_different_length_arcs_stay_distinct).

Stigmergic pheromone ranking: relevant_verified (skills.py:230) ranks by relevance then strength = success_rate * freshness * reuse_factor, where freshness = exp(-lambda * hours_since_updated_at). Verified trails are never deleted — disuse just evaporates their pull (test_skill_trail_evaporates_with_disuse: freshness drops <0.05 after 2000h, skill_id preserved).

Reuse pheromone (reinforcement on recall, separate from direct evidence): record_reuse (skills.py:163) credits/stains ONLY currently-verified trails that were recalled into a turn sharing its verdict; it NEVER writes success_count/failure_count, so reuse can re-rank but cannot launder a candidate into 'verified' (test_reuse_credit_cannot_promote_candidate).

Negative-pheromone quarantine: when reuse_failures - reuse_successes >= SKILL_REUSE_DEMOTE_NET (3), a verified trail is demoted to 'candidate' (quarantined); recovery requires fresh DIRECT verified success via record_attempt, and the reuse stain persists (test_reuse_failures_quarantine_verified_trail + test_direct_success_restores_quarantined_trail).

Evaporation-clock asymmetry: a reuse SUCCESS refreshes updated_at (keeps trail fresh); a reuse FAILURE deliberately does NOT — a misleading trail weakens AND keeps evaporating (test_reuse_success_refreshes_clock_failure_does_not).

Held-out-gated curriculum mastery: CurriculumManager (curriculum.py) locks higher levels until prior mastery; record_matching refuses anything not prefixed [VERIFY PASS]/[VERIFY FAIL] and cross-checks the passed flag against that evidence; _refresh_level masters a level only after training_passes_required passes AND coverage of every training task AND a held-out pass, then auto-unlocks level+1 (test_curriculum_requires_training_and_held_out_verifier_evidence).

Mistake pool with lifecycle + recurrence: MistakeMemory.record_or_increment (mistake.py:157) atomically de-dups active lessons by task+error and increments occurrence_count under BEGIN IMMEDIATE; lessons start 'pending', promote to 'verified', or 'supersede' with a lineage pointer; relevant_verified only surfaces verified lessons cross-session; confidence_delta is clamped to [-1.0, 0.0] so a lesson can only REDUCE planner confidence.

Confidence calibration loop: Planner._calibrate (planner.py:147) folds verified lessons (negative), verified skill strength (positive, capped by SKILL_CONFIDENCE_BONUS_MAX so a trail can never alone clear the human gate), and relevance-scaled historical success into the final per-step confidence — all wrapped in try/except so planning survives a memory outage.

Weighted developmental evidence: DevelopmentTracker.relevant_success_rate (development.py:73) computes a relevance-weighted success rate over ONLY verified_success/verified_failure events; summary() reports verification_coverage, human_intervention_rate, repeated_mistakes, etc.

Semantic vector memory: VectorIndex (embeddings.py:73) is an id-stable FAISS HNSW index (IndexIDMap binds each vector to semantic_memory.id, eliminating the legacy positional-coupling bug), cosine via normalised inner product, with cross-process mtime reload and atomic persist.

Privacy-preserving alignment evaluation: AlignmentEvaluationStore (alignment_evaluation.py) records per-turn understanding frames keyed by SHA-256 of the session id, validates all enums against allowlists, and summary() reports correction_rate / positive_feedback_rate / repeated_patterns with automatic_policy_updates hard-coded False (diagnostic only, never auto-acts).
sophistication: The stigmergy model is real and internally coherent, not decorative. _reuse_factor (skills.py:142) is a saturating, deliberately ASYMMETRIC pheromone function: factor = 1 + boost*(1-exp(-s/k_s)) - penalty*(1-exp(-f/k_f)), tuned so one failure bites harder than ~6-7 successes reward and failures saturate twice as fast (k_f=1.5 vs k_s=3.0). test_reuse_factor_exact_asymmetry pins the exact constants (0.70805 vs 1.04252). This is a designed loss-aversion prior, with config clamps that stop env tuning from inverting the asymmetry or dividing by zero.

Separation of DIRECT evidence (success_count/failure_count — the only thing that gates 'verified') from REUSE pheromone (reuse_*_count — ranking only) is a genuinely subtle correctness boundary, and it is enforced everywhere: record_reuse's docstring and code never touch direct counts, candidates can't be credited, and the migration recomputes status from direct counts alone. This prevents the classic failure mode where co-occurrence inflates trust.

The two-signature scheme is principled: the legacy exact signature is kept as lineage while signature_v2 strips arguments to merge redaction-noise siblings but preserves arc LENGTH because a longer arc is itself failure signal ('collapsing them would launder failures into verified trails' — relevance.py:52 docstring, enforced by test_different_length_arcs_stay_distinct). This shows the author understood a specific real-data fragmentation problem (secret-scanner redaction lives only in argument tails) and solved it without losing verdict information.

The migration in db.py:_migrate is production-grade defensive engineering: it backfills signature_v2 as a pure function of stored data (no clock => idempotent), consolidates fragmented trails by SUPERSEDING (never DELETEing — trail rows are 'irreplaceable verifier evidence') with a keeper-selection rule (verified > most-evidence > oldest), recomputes status honestly from summed direct counts (so absorbing a sibling's failures can correctly demote a previously-verified row — test_migration_prefers_verified_keeper), and creates the uniqueness index only after the column is guaranteed present. Three migration tests cover noop/merge/keeper-preference.

Recipe-quality refresh (skills.py:122): on a successful re-walk, the stored steps_json is replaced ONLY if the new steps carry fewer '<REDACTED:' artifacts — counts are never rewritten. This is a thoughtful detail because recalled steps are injected verbatim into future agent context, so a redacted step is a useless instruction.

FAISS id-stability via IndexIDMap binding vectors to semantic_memory.id (embeddings.py module docstring explicitly calls out fixing the legacy 'FAISS ordinal == SQLite row id' fragile assumption) plus mtime-based cross-process index reload (_refresh_if_changed) and atomic temp-file persist is careful multi-process design for a local-first app where multiple agent processes may read while one writes.

The trail_map introspection (skills.py:301) is a real observability surface: it computes per-trail freshness/reuse_factor/strength for ALL trails (candidates and quarantined included), distinguishes a reuse-demoted 'quarantined' trail from an honestly-weak candidate (status==candidate AND meets_promotion), lists superseded fragments with lineage, and emits the live constants — explicitly framed as the evidence base for tuning decay/reuse constants from real usage.

Concurrency correctness: every mutating recalculation (record_attempt, record_reuse, record_or_increment, curriculum.add_task/record_matching) runs under BEGIN IMMEDIATE to serialise concurrent reflection/agent workers, matching the WAL configuration.
subsystems.completeness: SOLID and proven, not scaffolded. This is the most mature subsystem I would expect in this repo: the data model, the promotion/demotion/quarantine state machine, the pheromone math, the migration, and the end-to-end loop wiring all exist and are exercised. The closed loop is real: _recall_skills injects verified workflows into agent context (main.py:1423), Planner._calibrate uses verified evidence to move confidence relative to the human-review gate (planner.py:184), and record_outcome (main.py:1485) writes development+skill+curriculum evidence keyed strictly off the turn's verifier verdict, then record_reuse credits/stains the recalled-but-not-rewalked trails (main.py:1530). gaps: Retrieval is purely lexical (relevance.py token-set cosine over a small stoplist) for skills, lessons, and development evidence. In practice a real user turn rarely equals a stored curriculum prompt verbatim, so live curriculum progression depends on prompts being issued near-identically — the mechanism is sound but its real-world trigger surface is narrow (consistent with the memory note that curriculum was live-proven via a controlled 6/6 run, not organic chat).

Pheromone/decay constants (SKILL_LAMBDA_DECAY=0.005/hr, reuse boost 0.15 / penalty 0.60, k_s=3.0 / k_f=1.5, demote-net=3) are reasoned defaults, not yet empirically tuned from accumulated production data — trail_map exists precisely to enable that tuning later, which is honest scaffolding-for-tuning rather than a stub.

VectorIndex is explicitly NOT internally serialised for concurrent mutation (docstring: callers must hold a cross-process lock and reload) — correctness depends on the caller (SemanticMemory) honoring that contract; it is a documented constraint, not a bug, but it is a sharp edge.
